intent: Docstring-only update in src/oida_code/operator_soak/aggregate.py to align rule-5 wording with the QA/A35 sec.5.8-F tightening (rules 3 and 4 short-circuit before rule 5 fires, so reaching rule 5 implicitly requires false_positive_count < 2 AND false_negative_count < 2). No functional change. No test change. No schema change.
notes:
Phase 5.8.1-B rerun on commit 2e19acf — supersedes earlier insufficient_fixture label.

History:
(1) run 24994865500 (27s, fail) — relative-path bug, fixed in commit 0b7a657.
(2) run 24995045522 (1m56s, success) — gateway-status=diagnostic_only, accepted=0, rejected=1 (C.docstring.no_behavior_delta rejected because pytest emitted no [E.tool.pytest.0] evidence). cgpro v1 labelled insufficient_fixture (UX 1/1/1/2).
(3) Phase 5.8.1 (commit da2bca1) — adapter patch: every error path emits a citable [E.tool.<binary>.0] diagnostic.
(4) Local verify-claims with synthetic post-patch packet revealed safety regression: 5.8.1 alone PROMOTED the false claim from rejected to accepted. Advisor flagged before any redispatch.
(5) Phase 5.8.1-B (commit 9b51def) — split diagnostic (citable, non-promoting) from actionable (citable, promoting) in the gateway loop. _enforce_requested_tool_evidence + _enforce_pass2_tool_citation key off actionable_evidence only.
(6) Recommendation regen (commit 2e19acf) — both enforcers now regenerate the report.recommendation string after demoting, so the JSON report's accepted/rejected/unsupported counts match the actual claim lists.
(7) run 25021820479 (1m45s, success) — gateway-status=diagnostic_only, accepted=0, rejected=0, unsupported=1, blocker='requested tool produced only diagnostic evidence (adapter status in error/timeout/tool_missing); cannot promote pass-2 claims (Phase 5.8.1-B)'. cgpro session unslop-md labelled useful_true_negative with UX 2/2/1/2.

Workflow topology bug (A) remains active and is documented in case_001_pytest_evidence_diagnosis.md — pytest still cannot find the test file at runtime because the dual-checkout layout puts the verifier's cwd at $GITHUB_WORKSPACE rather than oida-target/. Phase 5.8.1-B does NOT touch that bug; it only ensures the verifier's safety contract holds when pytest errors out. cgpro recommended fix-topology as the next blocker before treating any soak result as actionable evidence about the target code.

Forbidden-token scan over all 5 downloaded artefacts of run 25021820479 returned zero hits across (total_v_net, debt_final, corrupt_success, corrupt_success_ratio, verdict, merge_safe, merge-safe, production_safe, bug_free, verified, security_verified). ADR-22 hard wall holds.
